name: Daniels-Sheridan
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving rural Montana counties with checking, savings, loans, and mobile banking services since establishment in the Daniels-Sheridan region.
description_long:
Daniels-Sheridan Federal Credit Union (DSFCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union dedicated to serving the financial needs of residents across seven Montana counties: Daniels, Garfield, McCone, Phillips, Roosevelt, Sheridan, and Valley County. The credit union operates under NCUA insurance, providing the safety and protections typical of the credit union cooperative model. Their mission emphasizes "People Helping People," reflected in their approach to community banking and member services.

DSFCU offers a comprehensive suite of deposit and lending products. On the deposit side, they provide checking accounts (including rewards checking with Kasasa Cash and Visa Treat programs), savings accounts, youth accounts, money market accounts, share certificates, and individual retirement accounts (IRAs). Their lending products include auto and specialty vehicle loans (RVs and boats), agriculture and business loans, credit cards, personal loans, mortgages, and home equity lines of credit. Members can access these services through online banking with bill pay, a mobile banking app, and two physical branch locations.

What distinguishes DSFCU is their explicit commitment to rural Montana communities and agricultural lending. They specifically advertise agriculture and business loans alongside consumer products, suggesting meaningful support for the farming and ranching economies of their service area. The credit union also operates a "People Helping People Fund" and offers scholarship applications to members, indicating community investment beyond standard banking services. Their specialty vehicle loan focus (RVs and boats) reflects the lifestyle and recreation priorities of their regional membership.

As a regional credit union, DSFCU's main limitations are geographic scope and likely smaller product selection compared to large national banks. The website provides limited detail on rates, fees, or specific loan terms, requiring members to contact branches directly for pricing. Their service area is deliberately narrow, making this institution only accessible to residents of their seven-county Montana region. For those within their footprint, DSFCU represents a cooperative banking alternative focused on community and member benefit rather than shareholder returns.
services: Savings accounts with competitive deposit rates
Credit cards with Visa Treat rewards
Mortgages and home equity lines of credit
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure with profits returned to members rather than shareholders
NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 (standard for credit unions)
Dedicated agriculture and business lending products for rural entrepreneurs and farmers
Mobile banking app for 24/7 account access and management
Free rewards checking with Kasasa Cash program and Visa debit card
Two physical branch locations in Scobey and Medicine Lake for in-person service
Scholarship applications and People Helping People Fund showing community investment
cons: Limited to seven Montana counties—not accessible to out-of-region residents
Website lacks specific rates, fees, or loan terms; requires direct contact with branches
Small institution may have fewer product variations and features than national banks or larger credit unions
No mention of international banking, wire transfer services, or business checking products beyond agriculture loans
Limited digital product information makes it difficult to compare offerings before membership inquiry
best_for: Rural Montana residents in Daniels, Garfield, McCone, Phillips, Roosevelt, Sheridan, or Valley County
Farmers and agricultural business owners seeking specialized lending and cooperative banking
Individuals prioritizing community-focused banking and member-owned cooperative principles
RV and boat owners seeking specialty vehicle financing with flexible terms
diagnosis: DSFCU is ideal for individuals living in its seven-county Montana service area who value member-owned cooperative banking and need reliable consumer or agricultural lending. The main caveat is geographic exclusivity—membership and account opening are restricted to residents of Daniels, Garfield, McCone, Phillips, Roosevelt, Sheridan, or Valley County, Montana, making this institution inaccessible to consumers outside this rural region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
